# Beyond Passive Code Copiers: Embedding "AI Auditing" and Role-Play Simulations into Agile Software Engineering and Advanced Database Education

This repository contains the empirical datasets, data analytics pipelines, serious game configurations, and interactive simulation dashboard supporting the research paper: **"Beyond Passive Code Copiers: Embedding 'AI Auditing' and Role-Play Simulations into Agile Software Engineering and Advanced Database Education"** (TDTU IT Faculty, 2026).

The project is officially prepared and submitted for the **3rd Global Digital Intelligence Educational Innovation Competition (DI-IDEA 2026)** hosted by the _Global Digital Intelligence Development International Educational Alliance_ (Peking University Secretariat) and recommended by the _SMU AUN-TEPL_ thematic network.

## 📈 Key Empirical Metrics

Our framework transitions computing students from passive code copy-pasters into active, critical system auditors. Longitudinal evaluations over several cohorts (![](data:image/png;base64,)) yielded the following performance metrics:

- **Agile Scrum Role-Play Acceleration:** **3.83 / 5.0** mean sprint velocity improvement across Software Engineering teams.
- **Active Code Auditing Rate:** **12.8%** of students actively bypassed unoptimized ![](data:image/png;base64,) SQL suggestions and manually refactored logic, triggering the _Generation Effect_.
- **Affective Psychological Safety Net:** **4.28 / 5.0** mean comfort rating. Students leverage mobile devices as low-stakes sandboxes to mitigate evaluation anxiety.

## 📂 Repository Layout

beyond-passive-code-copiers/  
├── README.md # Main documentation & Quickstart  
├── LICENSE # MIT License  
├── requirements.txt # Python dependencies  
│  
├── data_pipeline/ # Google Colab Data Cleaning & Analytics  
│ ├── clean_pipeline.py # Robust CSV/TSV parser & regex integer extractor  
│ └── exploratory_analysis.ipynb # EDA notebook for generating Figures 1-5  
│  
├── agile_simulator/ # Serious Scrum Role-Play Game  
│ ├── run_simulation.py # CLI Sprint runner with automated event injects  
│ └── configs/  
│ ├── roles_prompts.json # Specialized system prompts for AI role-agents  
│ └── case_studies.json # Forklift warehousing & relational db schemas  
│  
├── auditing_protocols/ # "Desirable Difficulty" Curricular Materials  
│ ├── oracle_mutating_table/ # Triggers, ORA-04091 errors, and active audit guides  
│ ├── nested_loops_opt/ # O(N^2) query structures vs. CTE/Window optimization  
│ └── concurrency_lock/ # Transaction safety tasks (Missing FOR UPDATE triggers)  
│  
├── latex_paper/ # DI-IDEA 2026 Overleaf Source Files  
│ ├── di_idea_paper.tex # Complete XeLaTeX/LuaLaTeX paper  
│ └── figures/ # Saved PNG assets from Google Colab pipeline  
│  
└── web_demo/ # Interactive Web Sandbox  
└── index.html # Interactive Dashboard, AI Auditor & Sprint Sandbox

## 🧠 Theoretical Pillars of the Framework

Our educational design bypasses _automation bias_ and _performance-comprehension decoupling_ by anchoring the curriculum in four primary socio-technical and cognitive learning frameworks:

+-------------------------------------------------------+  
| Active Generation |  
| - counteracts cognitive offloading |  
| - triggers Bjork's "Desirable Difficulties" |  
+---------------------------+---------------------------+  
|  
v  
+---------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+  
| Distributed Cognition | Affective Filter Hypothesis |  
| - coordinates Agile human-AI teams | - lowers evaluation anxiety |  
| - maps Scrum role configurations | - utilizes private mobile interfaces |  
+---------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+

- **Cognitive (Active Generation):** Students are presented with faulty/hallucinated LLM code templates, forcing them to actively debug, write mental compilations, and verify outputs rather than passively accepting them.
- **Metacognitive (Self-Efficacy Model):** Breaking through the fear of long-term skill atrophy, successful "bug-busting" (e.g., resolving Oracle mutating tables) serves as Bandura's _Mastery Experiences_ to foster independent architecting confidence.
- **Emotional (Krashen's Affective Filter):** Shifting conceptual Q&A to private, non-judgmental mobile chatbots lowers learners' emotional barriers. This offsets the physical interface friction (extraneous cognitive load) logged on smartphones.
- **Process (Distributed Scrum):** Lightweight, gamified simulations teach empirical process control (transparency, inspection, adaptation) so students learn to manage changing conditions under collaborative flow.

## 🚀 Getting Started

### 1\. Requirements & Prerequisites

Ensure you have Python 3.10+ installed. Install the baseline dependencies:

pip install -r requirements.txt

### 2\. Data Cleaning & Figure Generation

Run the automated pipeline to clean raw CSV responses and reproduce the paper's descriptive distributions (Figures 1-5):

python data_pipeline/clean_pipeline.py

### 3\. CLI Agile Simulator

Experience lightweight sprint execution inside command terminals:

python agile_simulator/run_simulation.py --role TechLead --case forklift_warehouse

### 4\. Interactive Web Sandbox Demo

The web_demo/index.html file is a completely self-contained, serverless application.

- **To run locally:** Simply double-click web_demo/index.html or open it with any modern web browser.
- **Sandbox Features:** Includes an **AI Auditing Editor** to test real-world SQL bug refactoring, a **Sprint Role Simulator** to inspect system prompts, and **CLT/Sycophancy Modulators** to analyze the mobile ergonomics paradox in real-time.

## 📝 Citation & Academic Contact

If you use this pedagogical framework, code templates, or datasets in your educational research, please cite our paper:

@inproceedings{ ,  
author = {Faculty of Information Technology},
